sourceMovie.title: Ichi the Killer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ichi the Killer for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Takashi Miike keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 1
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ichi the Killer clicked for you, Kill Bill: Vol. 1 (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Sin City: A Dame to Kill For
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ichi the Killer clicked for you, Sin City: A Dame to Kill For (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ichi the Killer?
faq.answer: Try Kill Bill: Vol. 1, Sin City: A Dame to Kill For, Outrage—each shares DNA with Ichi the Killer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ichi the Killer?
faq.answer: Ichi the Killer (2001) is best described as Action, Crime, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ichi the Killer part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ichi Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
